settings: Short room or slapback only, very low send (~-24 dB). Keep it close.
reason: Place 'Lead Vocal' in the intimate depth layer.

settings: High-pass ~80 Hz. Cut ~250 Hz by 1-2 dB (mud). Avoid large 12 kHz+ air boosts (keeps the vocal human, not glossy).
reason: Clean low-end build-up and keep believable presence without hype.

settings: Vintage Opto, target 2-3 dB gain reduction, slow-ish attack.
reason: Stabilise the vocal while preserving the performance (invisible compression).

settings: Ride phrase endings +0.5 to +1 dB where the lyric drops, before adding compression.
reason: Ramone-style vocal belief: performance rides before brute-force compression.
validation: Check the gesture lands across the section boundary.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Acoustic Guitar' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: Cut 250-400 Hz by 1-2 dB.
reason: Reduce low-mid mud build-up.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Electric Guitar 1' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: Cut 250-400 Hz by 1-2 dB.
reason: Reduce low-mid mud build-up.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Electric Guitar 2' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: Cut 250-400 Hz by 1-2 dB.
reason: Reduce low-mid mud build-up.

settings: Hall or long plate, higher send (~-10 dB), filtered and diffuse.
reason: Place 'Synth Pad' in the background depth layer.

settings: High-pass ~250 Hz; low-pass the top if it is purely atmospheric.
reason: Filter felt elements so they support without crowding.

settings: Open the filter and lift the reverb send only in chorus sections; pull back in verses.
reason: Use the element as movement (felt), not a constant heard layer.
validation: Check the gesture lands across the section boundary.

settings: Hall or long plate, higher send (~-10 dB), filtered and diffuse.
reason: Place 'Splice Texture Loop' in the background depth layer.

settings: Narrow stereo width to ~35-50%.
reason: Felt/atmospheric element should not occupy the same width as heard elements.

settings: High-pass ~250 Hz; low-pass the top if it is purely atmospheric.
reason: Filter felt elements so they support without crowding.

settings: Consider chopping into transition gestures and muting the continuous bed; reserve for pre-chorus/bridge.
reason: Re-contextualise the imported loop instead of running it as a finished record inside the record.

settings: Open the filter and lift the reverb send only in chorus sections; pull back in verses.
reason: Use the element as movement (felt), not a constant heard layer.
validation: Check the gesture lands across the section boundary.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Bass' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: High-pass ~30 Hz; control 200-350 Hz if boxy.
reason: Tighten the low end without thinning the body.

settings: Vintage Opto, gentle 2-4 dB GR for consistent sustain.
reason: Even out the low-end foundation.

settings: Carve a small dip where the kick thumps (complementary to kick).
reason: Resolve kick/bass low-end conflict by carving, not stacking.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Kick' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: Shape sub (~50-70 Hz) vs beater (~3-5 kHz); leave room for bass.
reason: Define the kick without masking the bass.

settings: Short chamber, low send (~-18 dB).
reason: Place 'Snare' in the foreground depth layer.

settings: Light tonal shaping only; preserve transient detail and room realism.
reason: Halee naturalism: keep the kit believable.
